title: Introduction to React
author: Meta
text: React is a JavaScript library used for building interactive user interfaces through reusable components and a virtual DOM.

title: React Hooks
author: Meta
text: React Hooks allow functional components to manage state and side effects without using class components.

title: JavaScript Fundamentals
author: ECMA
text: JavaScript is a high-level programming language used for web development, server-side applications, and automation.

title: Node.js Runtime
author: OpenJS Foundation
text: Node.js is a runtime environment that enables JavaScript code to execute outside the browser using the V8 engine.

title: Express.js Framework
author: OpenJS Foundation
text: Express.js is a lightweight web framework for Node.js used to build APIs and server-side applications.

title: MongoDB Database
author: MongoDB Inc.
text: MongoDB is a NoSQL document database designed for scalability, flexibility, and high performance.

title: Redis Caching
author: Redis
text: Redis is an in-memory data store commonly used for caching, session management, and real-time analytics.

title: Docker Containers
author: Docker
text: Docker enables applications to run inside lightweight and portable containers with consistent environments.

title: Git Version Control
author: Git
text: Git is a distributed version control system used for tracking changes and collaborating on software projects.

title: REST APIs
author: Web Standards
text: REST is an architectural style for designing networked applications that communicate over HTTP.

title: GraphQL Basics
author: Meta
text: GraphQL is a query language for APIs that allows clients to request only the data they need.

title: TypeScript Overview
author: Microsoft
text: TypeScript is a statically typed superset of JavaScript that improves code quality and maintainability.

title: Next.js Framework
author: Vercel
text: Next.js is a React framework that provides server-side rendering, static generation, and routing features.

title: Authentication with JWT
author: RFC
text: JSON Web Tokens are used to securely transmit authentication and authorization information between systems.

title: Microservices Architecture
author: Software Engineering
text: Microservices divide an application into small independent services that communicate through APIs.
